payload.answer: With BRAIN_DEBUG_SQL_ENABLED=false, the system should not execute or expose the SQL result set for memory_cards. Therefore, there is no retrievable data here to reconcile into new or updated memory cards, and no durable memory should be committed.
payload.conflict_classification: configuration/visibility conflict (debug SQL disabled prevents retrieving memory_cards contents)

entity_resolution.reason: The fixture context indicates SQL debug is disabled; no actual memory_cards results or entities were provided to commit or reconcile.

payload.intent: Explain conflict arising from attempting to query memory cards when debug SQL is disabled.

details: No memory_cards content was provided in the input.
Given debug SQL is disabled, the query result cannot be assumed or reconstructed.
No durable memory changes were made.

repair_options: Enable BRAIN_DEBUG_SQL_ENABLED and re-run the SQL query to obtain memory_cards results, then re-evaluate and commit only verified facts.
Provide the memory_cards output directly in the input payload (without relying on SQL execution) so the assistant can safely extract and store facts.
Use a non-SQL memory retrieval interface (e.g., an API/tool call) intended to work with debug SQL disabled.
